title: IEC 81001-5-1 Security Risk Record
description: Records a security risk identified for a health software product built on MduX-rust — a threat/vulnerability pair and its assessed impact, controls, and residual-risk acceptance decision. Distinct from docs/iec62304/schemas/hazard.schema.json's safety Hazard: security risk and safety risk are related but not interchangeable concepts, see docs/iec81001/02-security-risk-management.md. Relevant to IEC 81001-5-1:2021's security risk management activity group (approx. §5) — clause numbering is a best-effort placeholder, see docs/iec81001/README.md. Not currently field-aligned with any crates/mdux-governance/src/lib.rs type; mdux-governance has no dedicated security-risk type today.

security_risk_id.description: e.g. 'SEC-001'. Sequential, unique across the security risk record set.

threat_description.description: What the threat is and how it could exploit a vulnerability to cause harm — the security-risk analogue of Hazard.description, but framed around a threat actor's deliberate action rather than a malfunction or foreseeable misuse.

affected_component.description: The crate, adapter, or tool this risk concerns, e.g. 'adapters/mdux-vulkan-winit' or 'crates/mdux-ml-authoring'. Free text rather than an enum, since it should stay in step with the workspace's actual crate map (docs/architecture.md) without requiring this schema to be updated whenever a crate is added.

attack_vector.description: How the threat would reach the affected component in practice, e.g. 'malformed .medui file supplied at build time' or 'corrupted generated/models/<id>/package.json substituted before deployment'.

impact.description: One or more classic CIA-triad impact categories this risk would affect if realized. An array, not a single enum, since a single threat commonly affects more than one property at once (e.g. a corrupted model package is both an Integrity and an Availability concern).

security_controls.description: Controls that mitigate this risk, as free text pointing at a real mechanism where one exists — e.g. 'ADR-005 trust-zone confinement', '#![forbid(unsafe_code)] in crates/mdux-ml-runtime', 'Classifier1D::new() fail-closed self-test (ADR-017)', 'tools/mdux-ml-baker verify byte-digest check (ADR-007)'. May be empty only if residual_risk_acceptable is being assessed with no controls yet identified; most records are expected to list at least one.

residual_risk_acceptable.description: Whether the risk remaining after the listed security_controls are applied is judged acceptable. A blunt boolean by design — see docs/iec81001/02-security-risk-management.md for why a real security risk file would carry a fuller rationale, likelihood/severity scoring, and review/approval trail alongside this field rather than relying on it alone.
